title_id: Hadis Kedua: Hubungan dengan Hadis Niat, Latar Belakang, dan Posisi Duduk Jibril
content_en: Some scholars point out the relationship between this hadith and the previous hadith, "Verily, all actions are but driven by intention." The previous hadith has a position like the statement, "In the name of Allah, the Compassionate, the Merciful," while this hadith has a position like surah al­ Faatiha. It is said that it is for this reason that scholars such as al-Baghawi began some of his works with the hadith about intention first and then this hadith in the same way that the Quran begins with the saying Bismillaah al­ Rahmaan al-Raheem followed by Surah al-Faatiha.3 According to ibn Hajr, this incident took place close to the Prophet's death. Some say that it was just before the Farewell Pilgrimage. Hence, it was as if the Prophet (peace be upon him), through the questioning of the angel Gabriel, was summarizing his mission and message. He summarized the essential concepts of imaan, islam and ihsaan. Then, at the end, he stated that the person was the Angel Gabriel who had come to teach them their religion.4 1 See Muhammad al-Sindi, Haashiyat al-Sindi ala Sunan al-Nasaai (Beirut: Daar Ihya al-Turaath al-Arabi, n.d.), vol. 8, p. 103. 2 Al-Qaadhi lyaadh al-Yahsoobi, Kitaab al-lmaan min Ikmaal al-Muallim (sic) bi-Fawaaid sahih Muslim (Riyadh: Daar al-Watn, 1417 A.H.), vol. 1 , p. 101. 3 Al-Baghawi did that in the beginning of his Sharh al-Sunnah as well as his Mishkat al-Sunnah ȋwhich was lȜter added on to and became Mishkat al-Masaabeeh). Cf., al-Qaan, vol. 1, p. 49. Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi The Circumstances Behind the Hadith In one of the narrations in Sahih Muslim, this hadith of Gabriel begins in the following manner: Abu Huraira narrated that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said, "Ask me [about matters related to the religion]." However, the people abstained from doing so because they were overawed out of profound respect for him. In the meanwhile a man came there and sat near his knees and said, "Messenger of Allah, what is Islam?" ... 1 According to al-Ubayy, the reason the Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Ask me," is because they were asking many questions and the Prophet (peace be upon him) realized that some were asking obstinately. Therefore, he became angry and said, "Ask me, ask me, for, by Allah, you will not ask me about anything except that I shall tell you about it as long as I am standing in this place."2 After hearing and seeing this, the people became fearful and refrained from asking any questions. When the people refrained from asking questions, Allah sent the angel Gabriel to put these important questions to the Prophet (peace be upon him).3 Al-Sanoosi adds that such questions do not go against the prohibition of asking questions. Actually, one must ask these types of questions because the answers to them are needed. Hence, this is an application of the Quranic verse, "So ask of those who know the Scripture, if you know not" (al-Nahl 43).4 In other words, there is a type of questioning that should be avoided and a type of questioning that is commanded. Questions of a useless nature or of a purely theoretical nature with no benefit to them are to be avoided. Questions for which answers are truly needed must be asked. Hence, Allah sent the angel Gabriel to the Prophet (peace be upon him) to ask him these questions and to demonstrate that important questions like these are to be asked of the people of knowledge. About The Narrator: Umar ibn al-Khattaab The narrator of this hadith is the same as the narrator of Hadith #1. Hence, his life was discussed earlier. 1 See Abdul Hameed Siddiqi, trans., Sahih Muslim, vol. 1, p. 4. 2 Actually, al-Ubayy is referring to another hadith in Sahih Muslim. 3 Abu Abdullah al-Ubayy, Sharh sahih Muslim (Riyadh: Maktaba Tabariyyah, n.d), vol. 1, pp. 77-78. 4 Muhammad al-Sanoosi, Sharh al-Sanusi, on the margin ofal-Ubayy, vol. 1, p. 77. I l I i j Hadith #2: The Hadith of Jibreel "One day while we were sitting with the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) ... put his hands on his thighs. He said, 'O Muhammad ... '" The different narrations of the hadith, referred to earlier in the takhreej section, describe the appearance of the angel Gabriel who came to the Prophet (peace be upon him) in the appearance of a bedouin Arab. Bedouins were known for their coarse and rough nature. The Angel spoke from the back of the mosque, "Peace be upon you, o Muhammad, may I come forward?" The Prophet (peace be upon him) told him to come forward and he continued asking until he came directly to the Prophet (peace be upon him), walking over the people as he came to the front of the mosque. He then sat directly in front of the Prophet (peace be upon him), putting his hands on the Prophet's thighs. Then he began to ask the Prophet (peace be upon him) his questions.
content_id:
Beberapa ulama menunjukkan hubungan antara hadis ini dengan hadis sebelumnya: 'Sesungguhnya semua amal bergantung pada niat.' Hadis sebelumnya memiliki kedudukan seperti kalimat 'Bismillahirrahmanirrahim', sementara hadis ini memiliki kedudukan seperti Surah al-Fatihah. Dikatakan bahwa itulah sebabnya ulama seperti al-Baghawi memulai sebagian karyanya dengan hadis tentang niat terlebih dahulu kemudian hadis ini, sebagaimana Al-Quran dimulai dengan Bismillahirrahmanirrahim dilanjutkan dengan Surah al-Fatihah.

Menurut Ibn Hajar, peristiwa ini terjadi menjelang wafatnya Nabi. Sebagian berpendapat ini terjadi tepat sebelum Haji Wada' (Haji Perpisahan). Dengan demikian seakan-akan Nabi, melalui pertanyaan-pertanyaan Malaikat Jibril, sedang merangkum misi dan pesannya. Beliau merangkum konsep-konsep esensial tentang iman, Islam, dan ihsan. Kemudian di akhir, beliau menyatakan bahwa orang tersebut adalah Malaikat Jibril yang datang untuk mengajarkan agama mereka kepada mereka.

Latar Belakang Hadis: Dalam salah satu riwayat Sahih Muslim, hadis Jibril ini dimulai dengan cara berikut: Abu Hurairah meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda: 'Tanyakanlah kepadaku [tentang perkara-perkara agama].' Namun orang-orang menahan diri karena mereka sangat kagum dan hormat kepada beliau. Sementara itu datanglah seorang laki-laki dan duduk di dekat lutut beliau lalu berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, apakah Islam itu?'

Menurut al-Ubayy, alasan Nabi bersabda 'Tanyakanlah kepadaku' adalah karena mereka banyak bertanya dan Nabi menyadari bahwa sebagian di antara mereka bertanya dengan sikap keras kepala. Karena itu beliau marah dan bersabda: 'Tanyakanlah kepadaku, tanyakanlah kepadaku! Demi Allah, tidaklah kalian menanyakan sesuatu kepadaku kecuali aku akan memberitahukan jawabannya selama aku masih berdiri di tempat ini.' Setelah mendengar dan menyaksikan hal itu, orang-orang menjadi takut dan menahan diri dari bertanya. Ketika orang-orang menahan diri dari bertanya, Allah mengutus Malaikat Jibril untuk mengajukan pertanyaan-pertanyaan penting tersebut kepada Nabi.

Al-Sanoosi menambahkan bahwa pertanyaan-pertanyaan seperti itu tidak bertentangan dengan larangan mengajukan pertanyaan. Bahkan seseorang harus mengajukan jenis pertanyaan ini karena jawabannya memang dibutuhkan. Dengan demikian ini adalah penerapan dari firman Allah: 'Maka bertanyalah kepada orang yang berilmu jika kamu tidak mengetahui' (al-Nahl: 43). Dengan kata lain, ada jenis pertanyaan yang harus dihindari dan ada jenis pertanyaan yang diperintahkan. Pertanyaan yang tidak berguna atau bersifat teoritis semata tanpa manfaat harus dihindari. Pertanyaan yang jawabannya benar-benar dibutuhkan harus diajukan. Karena itulah Allah mengutus Malaikat Jibril untuk mengajukan pertanyaan-pertanyaan ini kepada Nabi, sekaligus menunjukkan bahwa pertanyaan-pertanyaan penting seperti ini harus ditanyakan kepada para ulama.

Tentang Perawi: Umar ibn al-Khaththab — perawi hadis ini adalah orang yang sama dengan perawi Hadis #1, sehingga biografi beliau telah dibahas sebelumnya.

Komentar tentang Posisi Duduk Jibril: 'Suatu hari ketika kami sedang duduk bersama Rasulullah... meletakkan tangannya di atas pahanya. Ia berkata: Wahai Muhammad...' Berbagai riwayat hadis yang telah disebutkan sebelumnya dalam bagian takhrij menggambarkan penampilan Malaikat Jibril yang datang kepada Nabi dalam wujud seorang Arab badui. Orang-orang badui dikenal dengan sifat mereka yang kasar dan keras. Malaikat itu berbicara dari belakang masjid: 'Assalamu'alaika, wahai Muhammad, bolehkah aku maju ke depan?' Nabi mempersilakannya maju, dan ia terus meminta izin hingga datang langsung kepada Nabi, melangkahi orang-orang dalam perjalanannya menuju bagian depan masjid. Kemudian ia duduk tepat di hadapan Nabi, meletakkan tangannya di atas paha Nabi, lalu mulai mengajukan pertanyaan-pertanyaannya kepada Nabi.
content_ar: Some العلماء point out the relationship between this الحديث and the previous الحديث, "Verily, all actions are but driven by intention." The previous الحديث has a position like the statement, "In the name of الله, the Compassionate, the Merciful," while this الحديث has a position like surah al­ Faatiha. It is said that it is for this reason that العلماء such as al-Baghawi began some of his works with الحديث النبوي الشريف about intention first and then this الحديث in the same way that القرآن الكريم begins with the saying Bismillaah al­ Rahmaan al-Raheem followed by Surah al-Faatiha.3 According to ibn Hajr, this incident took place close to the Prophet's death. Some say that it was just before the Farewell الحج. Hence, it was as if النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him), through the questioning of the angel Gabriel, was summarizing his mission and message. He summarized the essential concepts of الإيمان, الإسلام and ihsaan. Then, at the end, he stated that the person was the Angel Gabriel who had come to teach them their religion.4 1 See Muhammad al-Sindi, Haashiyat al-Sindi ala Sunan al-Nasaai (Beirut: Daar Ihya al-Turaath al-Arabi, n.d.), vol. 8, p. 103. 2 Al-Qaadhi lyaadh al-Yahsoobi, Kitaab al-lmaan min Ikmaal al-Muallim (sic) bi-Fawaaid sahih المسلم (Riyadh: Daar al-Watn, 1417 A.H.), vol. 1 , p. 101. 3 Al-Baghawi did that in the beginning of his Sharh al-السنة as well as his Mishkat al-السنة ȋwhich was lȜter added on to and became Mishkat al-Masaabeeh). Cf., al-Qaan, vol. 1, p. 49. Commentary on the Forty الحديث of النووي The Circumstances Behind الحديث النبوي الشريف In one of the narrations in Sahih المسلم, this الحديث of Gabriel begins in the following manner: أبو هريرة رضي الله عنه روى that رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) said, "Ask me [about matters related to the religion]." However, the people abstained from doing so because they were overawed out of profound respect for him. In the meanwhile a man came there and sat near his knees and said, "Messenger of الله, what is الإسلام?" ... 1 According to al-Ubayy, the reason النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) said, "Ask me," is because they were asking many questions and النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) realized that some were asking obstinately. Therefore, he became angry and said, "Ask me, ask me, for, by الله, you will not ask me about anything except that I shall tell you about it as long as I am standing in this place."2 After hearing and seeing this, the people became fearful and refrained from asking any questions. When the people refrained from asking questions, الله sent the angel Gabriel to put these important questions to النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him).3 Al-Sanoosi adds that such questions do not go against the prohibition of asking questions. Actually, one must ask these types of questions because the answers to them are needed. Hence, this is an application of the Quranic verse, "So ask of those who know the Scripture, if you know not" (al-Nahl 43).4 In other words, there is a type of questioning that should be avoided and a type of questioning that is commanded. Questions of a useless nature or of a purely theoretical nature with no benefit to them are to be avoided. Questions for which answers are truly needed must be asked. Hence, الله sent the angel Gabriel to النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) to ask him these questions and to demonstrate that important questions like these are to be asked of the people of knowledge. About The روى: Umar ibn al-Khattaab The روى of this الحديث is the same as the روى of الحديث #1. Hence, his life was discussed earlier. 1 See Abdul Hameed Siddiqi, trans., Sahih المسلم, vol. 1, p. 4. 2 Actually, al-Ubayy is referring to another الحديث in Sahih المسلم. 3 Abu Abdullah al-Ubayy, Sharh sahih المسلم (Riyadh: Maktaba Tabariyyah, n.d), vol. 1, pp. 77-78. 4 Muhammad al-Sanoosi, Sharh al-Sanusi, on the margin ofal-Ubayy, vol. 1, p. 77. I l I i j الحديث #2: الحديث النبوي الشريف of Jibreel "One day while we were sitting with رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) ... put his hands on his thighs. He said, 'O Muhammad ... '" The different narrations of الحديث النبوي الشريف, referred to earlier in the takhreej section, describe the appearance of the angel Gabriel who came to النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) in the appearance of a bedouin Arab. Bedouins were known for their coarse and rough nature. The Angel spoke from the back of the المسجد, "Peace be upon you, o Muhammad, may I come forward?" النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) told him to come forward and he continued asking until he came directly to النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him), walking over the people as he came to the front of the المسجد. He then sat directly in front of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him), putting his hands on the Prophet's thighs. Then he began to ask النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) his questions.
